pages.full_text:
Sat. July 21st (The day after our 38th Wedding Anniversary) Simon + Eve set off at 4 pm for Italy. We called in at Chad for Simon to set up the ansaphone with instructions for the holiday. We are listening to the radio - Tour de France ends in Paris tomorrow. John has texted from Kent, on his way to Paris now, that he has had his offer - 272,000 accepted on the Mumbles House. Very happy. Charles is house hunting to rent within 40 minutes of Warwick. Then he's going on holiday to Germany + meeting Jenny. Peter is filming in the desert outside Urumchi. William + Anjony are staying in Somerset then taking Tess to see the farm. Thomas + Naomi are taking the children to France. John will be doing physiological testing at high altitude in Chile.
8 We arrived in __Stallisfield__ again, the same as last year. The pub runs a casino in huts across the road. A nice little campsite. We went for a walk. Simon has had a long day 7 haircut with Toni, 8.30 set of to pick up Helen + take her to
pages.notes: Year not stated on page. 'Sat. July 21st' and 'Tour de France ends in Paris tomorrow' (i.e. ends Sunday July 22nd) could help identify the year. 'Anjony' may be 'Anjony' or a name variant; could also read as 'Anjory'. '8 We arrived' — the '8' may be a marginal page number or paragraph marker. 'Stallisfield' is underlined; could be 'Stalisfield' (village in Kent). 'Urumchi' is likely Ürümqi, China.

pages.full_text:
Margaret at Sunrise. Mowed all the lawns. 3 picked up Helen + sent her home in a taxi. A relief to be here.
__Sunday July 22nd.__
	Went for bike ride from Charing. Very pretty village shops. We missed 9.30 church service, invited to coffee before 11 youth service in barn. Friendly. Left before service. Queues at Channel tunnel terminus. Irate English woman from Dutch car at foreign bus. The wait was long + stressful + during the trip the van (a small VW) shunted into the back of us. We pushed it away + no damage done. 1 hour late.
	We have stopped for tea at an Aire near lille. A man is cooking on a blue gas bottle outside behind the cab, a flame right next to the gas tank. He's in a [?] [illegible] Czech lorry. There are also Czech lorries + a row of identical Portugese lorries with holy mascots.
	It took ages to find the Belgican campsite - lots of windy roads up a hill to St. Seveur. Finally we got there, only to find that it had
pages.notes: Year not stated on page. 'Belgican campsite' is the diarist's spelling (likely 'Belgian campsite'). 'St. Seveur' may be 'St. Sever' or a similar French place name — could not confirm exact spelling from text. 'lille' written with lower case l. 'Portugese' is the diarist's spelling of 'Portuguese'. The phrase 'He\'s in a [?] [illegible] Czech lorry' — a word or two between 'a' and 'Czech' is unclear; possibly 'either' or 'a hired'. Handwritten page number 10 visible in top-right corner. Page text continues beyond the bottom of the page.
